questions.question: What is the purpose of a Software Requirement Specification (SRS) document?
keyPoints: To ensure that all stakeholders are on the same page regarding the software's functionality, performance, and quality

questions.question: What are the benefits of a well-written SRS document?
keyPoints: Ensures that the software meets the requirements

questions.question: What is the difference between functional and non-functional requirements?
keyPoints: Functional requirements describe what the system should do
Non-functional requirements describe how the system should behave

questions.question: Explain the importance of prioritizing requirements in SRS.
keyPoints: To ensure that the most critical requirements are met first

questions.question: Describe the role of SRS in software development.
keyPoints: SRS serves as a guide for software development, testing, and maintenance
